title: Share dialog uses dynamic Tailwind size classes that may not be generated
reasoning: Tailwind class extraction depends on statically visible class names. Building class names like h-${size} and w-${size} can omit required avatar dimensions from the generated CSS, especially for values that are only present through this helper. That causes share-dialog avatars or pending-user chips to render with missing width/height in production builds.
recommendation: Replace the dynamic class construction with an explicit size map, for example {6: 'h-6 w-6', 8: 'h-8 w-8'}, or use inline styles for dimensions.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked tests render TipTapRenderer only; they do not build Tailwind output or render ShareDialog avatar sizes.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a ShareDialog render test for pending-user and search-result avatars, or a build/CSS smoke test that verifies the generated stylesheet includes the avatar size classes used by this component.
minimumFixScope: Change the local Avatar helper in ShareDialog.tsx to use statically enumerable size classes.
